workflow_name: PE Workforce Due Diligence Assessment (30-Day Engagement)
description: Standard 30-day engagement workflow for assessing target company data/AI team capability. Phases: Scoping → Data Collection → Assessment → Analysis → Delivery.

objectives: Align on value creation plan and assessment scope
Set expectations for target company cooperation

participants.client: PE deal sponsor (operating partner or investment manager)
optional: target CFO or HR lead (context-setting only)

agenda: Review deal thesis and value creation plan
Discuss data/AI role in value drivers
Clarify assessment scope (which teams, which roles)
Explain WIDAI methodology at high level
Confirm timeline and target company contact(s)
decision_points: Is value creation plan clearly defined? (If not, defer assessment or scope narrowly)

activities.activity_name: Value Creation Plan & Reference Architecture Review
activities.description: Assessor reviews deal materials: operating plan, value creation thesis, relevant team org charts. Maps value drivers (e.g., 'build ML pricing model', 'improve data governance') to WIDAI role requirements. Selects reference architecture (Early-Stage, Growth, Scaled, Enterprise).
inputs: Value creation plan (provided by PE firm)
decision_points: Which reference architecture is closest fit? (Determines baseline expectations)
What are the 3-5 critical roles for this specific deal? (Inputs criticality scoring later)

activities.description: Assessor prepares data request package for target company: org chart template, role description template, team profile template, evidence checklist (certifications, work samples). Request is sent to PE firm for transmission to target.
activities.delivery_method: Email from PE firm sponsor to target company (or target HR/IT contact identified in A1-1)
activities.key_messaging: Request emphasizes: (1) 30-day timeline, (2) confidentiality, (3) data will inform integration planning and talent strategy, (4) 2-hour time commitment per person for optional interviews

activities.description: Brief call with target company to clarify data request, answer questions, confirm timeline. Sets tone for cooperation. Optional but recommended for complex orgs.
key_messages: Explain WIDAI assessment at high level (what it measures, what it doesn't)
Offer to walk through templates if helpful
decision_points: Is target company willing to provide data on time? (If not, defer assessment)
Are there sensitive roles or information restrictions?

phase_decision_gate.gate_question: Do we have a clear value creation plan, reference architecture, and committed target company cooperation?
proceed_criteria: Value creation plan is documented (even if preliminary)
Target company has been sent data request
Target company has confirmed willingness to participate
block_criteria: Value creation plan is vague or absent
Target company is uncooperative or timeline-constrained
phase_decision_gate.blocked_action: Defer assessment to later date; recommend PE firm clarify value creation thesis first

phases.phase_summary: Scoping phase ensures alignment on deal thesis, establishes assessment scope, and triggers target company data collection. By end of Phase 1, assessor has selected reference architecture and target company is providing data.

objectives: Receive target company org chart and team profiles
Map each person to WIDAI roles

activities.description: Assessor receives data package from target company. Validates completeness: org chart, role descriptions, team profiles, evidence artifacts (LinkedIn profiles, resumes, certifications, work samples).
data_completeness_checklist: Org chart includes reporting structure and headcount
Team profile includes name, title, years in role, years in company
Evidence artifacts are sufficient for KSA assessment (certifications, work history, project descriptions)
decision_points: Is data complete enough to proceed? (If not, request follow-up within 48 hours)
Are there any data quality issues (missing titles, unclear reporting lines)? (Flag for follow-up)

activities.description: Assessor maps each person in target org to 1-2 WIDAI roles based on title, responsibilities, and reporting structure. Uses fuzzy matching: person's job title → WIDAI canonical role. Records primary role and (if applicable) secondary role.
mapping_process: 1. Extract job title from org chart
2. Parse role description (responsibilities, reports-to, team size managed)
3. Match against WIDAI role inventory (GOV, ENG, DEV, ANL, OPS, RSK, REG, LDR, DSM, NICHE)
4. Assign primary role (most likely) and secondary role (if person has dual responsibilities)
5. Record confidence level (high/medium/low) for each mapping
fuzzy_matching_examples: 'Data Engineering Manager' → WIDAI-ENG-0005 (Senior Data Engineer, assuming lead responsibilities)
'VP Data' → WIDAI-ENG-0001 (Head of Data Engineering / Chief Data Engineer) or WIDAI-GOV-0002 (Chief Data Officer, depending on scope)
decision_points: Are there roles in target org that don't map to WIDAI? (Flag as coverage gap; may indicate specialized role or new role type)
Are there ambiguous mappings? (Use secondary role or document uncertainty)
outputs: Role mapping matrix (person → WIDAI role, with confidence)

activities.description: For each mapped role, assessor compiles evidence of person's KSA attainment. Evidence sources: certifications, work history (LinkedIn), project descriptions (from role description), work samples (code repos, dashboards, reports), educational background.
evidence_hierarchy: Certification (IAPP CIPM, ISACA CDPSE, cloud certifications, etc.)
Work experience (projects, years in similar role, company progression)
Interview/assessment (optional; optional technical interview or problem-solving scenario)
evidence_collection_process: 1. Identify WIDAI KSAs required for person's primary role
2. Extract evidence from provided artifacts (resumes, LinkedIn, certifications, work samples)
3. Compile in structured evidence template (one template per person)
4. Flag gaps: KSAs with no evidence
5. Recommend optional interviews for high-criticality roles with weak evidence
outputs: Capability evidence matrix (person × KSA, with evidence)

activities.description: For roles with high criticality and weak capability evidence, assessor recommends optional interviews (3-5 people). Interviews are brief (30-45 min) and targeted: assess depth of KSA knowledge, not performance evaluation.
interview_recommendation_criteria: Role criticality ≥4 AND capability evidence is weak (weak evidence on 3+ KSAs)
Role is key person risk candidate (concentration risk suspected)
Role is gate-item for value creation plan
activities.recommended_interview_count: 3-5 people (typically CDAO, top engineers, ML lead, governance lead)
activities.interview_depth: 30-45 minutes per person. Format: open-ended questions about experience, technical depth, understanding of org data architecture/ML roadmap.
example_interview_questions: Walk me through the data architecture you inherited / built. What are the key components? (Tests data architecture knowledge)
Describe your experience with [relevant technology from KSAs]. What scale? What challenges? (Tests hands-on expertise)
If you left the company tomorrow, what would be hardest to replace? (Tests concentration risk awareness)
outputs: Interview recommendation memo (names, reasons, suggested questions)
activities.decision_point: Does target company agree to interviews? (Ideal but not required; assessment proceeds with desk-based evidence if interviews not possible)

phase_decision_gate.gate_question: Do we have sufficient data to score all mapped roles?
proceed_criteria: All people are mapped to WIDAI roles
Capability evidence is collected for all primary roles
block_criteria: Org chart is incomplete (missing roles or people)
Evidence is too sparse to score capability (no certifications, no work history)
phase_decision_gate.blocked_action: Request follow-up from target company; delay scoring until data is complete

phases.phase_summary: Data collection phase gathers org structure, maps people to WIDAI roles, and compiles capability evidence. By end of Phase 2, assessor has complete data for scoring.

objectives: Score each role on coverage, capability, and criticality
Calculate concentration risk for each person

activities.description: For each mapped role, score coverage: is role filled, at what seniority, at what FTE? Reference architecture defines expected coverage per role. Scoring model: coverage_score = (actual_headcount / reference_headcount) × seniority_adjustment × fte_adjustment.
inputs: Org chart and person data (from A2-1)
scoring_process: 1. For each role in reference architecture:
2. Count actual headcount in target org for that role
3. Compare to reference architecture expectation (e.g., 'expect 2.0 FTE Data Engineers')
4. Assess seniority: is actual person at required seniority? (adjust if above/below)
5. Record FTE allocation (full-time, part-time, shared role)

activities.description: For each person, score capability: do they have the KSAs required for their mapped role? Score based on evidence hierarchy (certifications, academic, work experience, optional interview).
scoring_process: 1. For each person and their mapped WIDAI role:
2. Extract WIDAI KSA requirements for that role
3. For each KSA, assess evidence (certification, work history, interview)
5. Calculate KSA_attainment_score = (KSAs with evidence / total KSAs required)
6. Calculate capability_score = KSA_attainment × evidence_quality_weight + interview_depth (if conducted)
evidence_strength_weights: Strong evidence (direct certification or work project): 1.0
Moderate evidence (related experience or partial indicator): 0.7
Weak evidence (general background, no direct indicator): 0.4

activities.description: For each role, assign criticality: how critical is this role to achieving the value creation plan? Criticality is specific to this deal (not generic). Scored 1-5; higher = more critical.
criticality_assignment_process: 1. Review value creation plan: identify 3-5 main value drivers
2. For each role, map to value drivers: which drivers depend on this role?
3. Assign criticality level (1-5) based on impact:
- 5: Single point of failure; role is irreplaceable; loss = deal re-scope
- 4: Essential; on critical path; loss requires external hire
- 3: Core; supports value creation; loss creates delay
- 2: Supporting; important but not critical path

activities.description: For each person, score concentration risk: how much unique knowledge is held by this person? How hard to replace? What's the flight risk? Composite = unique_knowledge × replacement_difficulty × tenure_risk.
inputs: Evidence of unique knowledge (CODEOWNERS, architecture docs, etc.)

concentration_risk_components.assessment: What % of org's domain knowledge is held exclusively by this person?
How long to backfill this role with external hire?
Years in role/company + flight risk indicators

outputs: Top key person risk report (top 5 people by score)

participants.assessor: WIDAI assessment lead or data analyst

activities.description: Aggregate individual scores into three composite indices: Team Capability Index (TCI), Key Person Risk Score (KPRS), and Gap Severity Index (GSI). Then compute final Workforce Readiness Score (WRS).

composite_score_calculations.description: Weighted average of capability across all roles, weighted by criticality

composite_score_calculations.formula: KPRS = max(concentration_risk_i) if N_high_risk > 0, else avg(concentration_risk_i)
composite_score_calculations.description: Identifies single highest concentration risk; if multiple high-risk people, captures max
Quantifies unfilled/under-filled roles, weighted by criticality
Final composite score; primary deliverable to PE investment committee

outputs: Composite score report (TCI, KPRS, GSI, WRS)

activities.description: Classify WRS into band (Critical, At-Risk, Adequate, Strong, Exceptional) and interpret implications for PE investment committee.

band_classification.interpretation: Team not ready; deal thesis at material risk
band_classification.pe_signal: RED FLAG: Recommend deal termination or major workforce overhaul
PROCEED WITH PLANNING: Standard execution plan

outputs: WRS band classification and PE signal

phase_decision_gate.gate_question: Are all scores defensible and traceable to evidence?
proceed_criteria: All roles are scored on coverage, capability, criticality
Every score can be traced back to evidence or reference architecture
validation_checks: Coverage scores sum to reference headcount (or less if under-staffed)
Capability scores correlate with evidence strength
Criticality assignments are aligned with value creation plan
Concentration risk identifies realistic key person dependencies

phases.phase_summary: Assessment phase scores all dimensions (coverage, capability, criticality, concentration risk) and produces composite indices. By end of Phase 3, WRS and key findings are determined.

objectives: Analyze gaps and create hiring priorities
Identify top key person risks and mitigation strategies
Produce four deliverables (Executive Summary, Workforce Map, Gap Analysis, Key Person Risk Assessment)

activities.description: Analyze unfilled and under-filled roles. For each gap, recommend hiring sequence, required seniority, and WIDAI job description seed.
gap_analysis_process: 2. Rank by: (1 - coverage_score) × criticality_weight (gap impact × role importance)
- Required seniority level and years of experience
- Key KSA profile (from WIDAI role definition)
- Estimated market scarcity (using WIDAI job market data)
- Hiring timeline (how urgent given value creation plan)
4. Sequence hiring: critical-path roles first, support functions later

hiring_priority_output.rationale: Pipeline architecture is critical to real-time ML model; currently understaffed
hiring_priority_output.required_profile: 5+ years data engineering; experience with Spark, Kafka, cloud warehousing

outputs: Hiring priority matrix (ranked by gap impact × criticality)

activities.description: For top 3-5 concentration risk people, develop mitigation recommendations: retention bonuses, knowledge documentation, succession planning.

mitigation_strategies.target: High concentration risk + high criticality + early-tenure
mitigation_strategies.rationale: Reduce flight risk during PE transition
mitigation_strategies.example_structure: 12-month or 24-month equity vest or cash bonus, tied to staying through defined milestones

mitigation_strategies.target: High concentration risk regardless of criticality
mitigation_strategies.example: Architecture docs, runbooks, CODEOWNERS handoff, lunch-and-learns for team
Pair with junior engineer; mentor 1-2 people over 6 months

mitigation_strategies.target: High concentration risk + gap in market availability
mitigation_strategies.example: Hire consultant for first 3-6 months while recruiting permanent replacement

outputs: Key person risk mitigation plan (for each top 5 risk person)

activities.description: Produce 1-2 page executive summary for investment committee. Includes: WRS score, TCI/KPRS/GSI breakdown, 3 key findings, go/no-go signal, top 3 hiring priorities.
executive_summary_sections: - Finding 1: (e.g., 'Team is strong in data engineering but lacks ML capability')
- Finding 2: (e.g., 'CDO is single point of failure for governance')
- Finding 3: (e.g., '3 critical roles are unfilled; search timeline is 6+ months')
3. Investment Committee Signal: (e.g., 'Proceed with standard planning. Team is adequate. Monitor CDO retention. Plan hiring for ML engineer and data analysts.')
4. Top 3 Hiring Priorities with estimated timeline and market difficulty
5. Key Person Risk Summary: (top 2-3 concentration risks with mitigation)
outputs: Executive Summary (1-2 pages, formatted for board presentation)

activities.description: Produce 5-10 page Workforce Capability Map: every team member mapped to WIDAI roles, with coverage and capability scores visualized as heat map.
capability_map_sections: 1. Org Chart with WIDAI Role Overlays (visual): Each person → WIDAI role, color-coded by coverage/capability band (Red=<50%, Yellow=50-75%, Green=75%+)
2. Team Summary Table: All people, with primary role, coverage score, capability score, criticality, concentration risk
3. Role-Level Heat Map: Each role from reference architecture, with current coverage, capability, and criticality
4. KSA Coverage Analysis: For each role, which KSAs are covered by the team, which are gaps?
5. Supporting narrative: Brief description of team strengths and gaps
visual_elements: Org chart with WIDAI role labels
Capability score heat map (person × role capability)
KSA coverage by role (stacked bar chart)
outputs: Workforce Capability Map (5-10 pages, heavily visual)

activities.description: Produce 3-5 page Gap Analysis & Hiring Priority Plan: unfilled roles, under-filled roles, capability gaps, ranked by impact. Includes job description seeds from WIDAI role definitions.
inputs: WIDAI role definitions (with KSA and job description seeds)
gap_analysis_sections: 1. Gap Summary Table: All gaps ranked by (1 - coverage) × criticality; includes role, gap type (unfilled/under-filled), impact, timeline
2. Critical Hiring Priority List: Top 5-7 roles to hire, with recommended profile and market difficulty
3. WIDAI Job Description Seeds: For each recommended hire, starting job description sourced from WIDAI role definition
4. Hiring Timeline & Resource Plan: Recruiting timeline, required seniority, estimated salary range (from WIDAI job market data, if available)
5. Alternative Approaches: For hard-to-fill roles, recommend: internal upskilling, consultant bridge, outsource to vendor
outputs: Gap Analysis & Hiring Priority Plan (3-5 pages)

activities.description: Produce 2-3 page Key Person Risk Assessment: top 5 concentration risks, mitigation strategies, succession planning inputs.
key_person_risk_sections: 1. Key Person Risk Summary: Top 5 people by concentration risk; risk level (high/moderate/low); key knowledge areas
2. Individual Risk Profiles: For each top 5 person:
- Name, title, primary role, years in company
- Concentration risk score and components (unique knowledge %, replacement difficulty, tenure risk)
- Knowledge areas held exclusively by this person
3. Mitigation Recommendations: For each high-risk person, recommended retention strategy, knowledge documentation plan, succession plan
4. Succession Planning Roadmap: Timeline for developing backup capabilities
5. Retention Strategy: Recommended incentives, milestone-based vesting, key person insurance

activities.description: Internal quality check: verify all scores are traceable, findings are supported by evidence, narrative is clear, deliverables are complete.
quality_checklist: Are all composite scores (TCI, KPRS, GSI, WRS) clearly documented and defensible?
Are key findings supported by specific evidence (coverage gaps, capability shortfalls, concentration risks)?
Are hiring recommendations realistic given market scarcity and timeline?
Are all four deliverables complete and professionally formatted?
Is the executive summary actionable for investment committee?
Are there any factual errors or inconsistencies?
outputs: Deliverable package (4 documents): (1) Executive Summary, (2) Workforce Capability Map, (3) Gap Analysis, (4) Key Person Risk Assessment

phase_decision_gate.gate_question: Are all four deliverables complete, accurate, and ready for investment committee?
proceed_criteria: Executive Summary is 1-2 pages with clear WRS, key findings, and go/no-go signal
Workforce Capability Map shows all team members mapped to WIDAI roles
Gap Analysis prioritizes hiring and provides WIDAI job description seeds
Key Person Risk Assessment identifies top risks and mitigation strategies

phases.phase_summary: Analysis phase produces gap analysis, hiring priorities, and four deliverables. By end of Phase 4, assessment is ready for delivery to PE investment committee.

objectives: Present assessment to PE investment committee
Confirm next steps (hiring, integration planning, key person retention)

participants.audience: PE investment committee (typical: investment manager, operating partner, deal sponsor)

activities.description: Present assessment findings to investment committee. Focus: WRS score, key findings, hiring priorities, key person risks, next steps.
presentation_agenda: 1. (10 min) Methodology overview: What WIDAI measures, what it doesn't; reference architecture used
2. (15 min) Workforce Readiness Score (WRS): Overall assessment, band classification, PE signal
3. (10 min) Team Capability Index (TCI): Strengths and gaps by role/function
4. (10 min) Gap Severity Index (GSI): Unfilled roles, hiring priorities, timeline
5. (10 min) Key Person Risk Score (KPRS): Top 3-5 concentration risks, mitigation recommendations
6. (15 min) Workforce Capability Map: Visual walkthrough of team strengths and gaps
7. (10 min) Hiring Plan: Top 5 priorities, market difficulty, estimated timeline
8. (10 min) Next Steps: Succession planning, retention bonuses, integration roadmap
key_messaging: This is not a performance evaluation — it's an assessment of team readiness for the value creation plan
Scores are based on observable evidence (certifications, work history, WIDAI KSA requirements), not subjective judgment
Gaps are addressable; the assessment tells you where to focus hiring and development

activities.description: Open discussion: answer questions about methodology, explore findings, discuss implications for integration plan and valuation.
common_questions: Why is this role scored as critical? (Answer: Because it directly supports [value driver])
Can we hire quickly for this role? (Answer: Market data suggests 3-6 month search; here's the profile)
What if [key person] leaves? (Answer: That's the concentration risk score; here's the mitigation plan)
How confident are you in this score? (Answer: Score is based on [evidence]; confidence is [high/medium] because [evidence quality])

activities.description: Confirm next steps: when will hiring begin, how will key person retention be handled, when will integration planning start, what follow-up is needed from WIDAI.
next_steps_topics: Integration planning timeline (when does integration team stand up?)
Hiring plan execution (who owns recruiting for the gaps?)
Key person retention (when do retention conversations happen?)
Follow-up from WIDAI (post-diligence methodology refinement, 90-day check-in, etc.)
Confidentiality and data handling (assessment findings are sensitive; confirm how they'll be shared/protected)

phases.phase_summary: Delivery phase presents assessment to investment committee, answers questions, and confirms next steps for integration planning.

data_quality: Org chart is complete and unambiguous
All people are mapped to WIDAI roles with documented rationale
Capability evidence is sufficient for scoring (not purely anecdotal)
No critical data gaps or conflicts
scoring_rigor: All scores are defensible and traceable to evidence
Coverage scores align with org chart headcount
Capability scores reflect KSA evidence, not personality assessment
Criticality assignments are specific to value creation plan, not generic
Concentration risk identification is realistic (based on knowledge distribution, not just tenure)
deliverable_quality: Executive Summary is concise and actionable
Workforce Capability Map is clear and visual
Gap Analysis provides specific hiring recommendations
Key Person Risk Assessment includes credible mitigation strategies
pe_readiness: Findings are in PE language (investment thesis, value drivers, risk signals)
Recommendations are practical (can be implemented in 30-90 day timeline)

common_variations.compressed_timeline: If PE needs assessment in <30 days: skip interviews (use desk-based evidence only), parallel phases where possible, reduce depth of Gap Analysis. Minimum viable scope: WRS score + Executive Summary + Hiring Priorities.
common_variations.large_organization: If target has 100+ person data/AI team: may need to assess by function (e.g., separate assessments for Data Engineering, ML/AI, Analytics, Governance). Adjust reference architecture to reflect functional structure.
common_variations.special_situations: If target company is in sensitive industry (healthcare, defense, finance): may need additional controls for data handling, security clearances, or regulatory review. Adjust phase 1 accordingly.
